contract.rationale: backdated_ledger_events revokes anon/authenticated access; the script uses a service credential but exposes only a local SELECT helper and calls no mutation or RPC methods.

backdated_fingerprint_clarification.rationale: A sale-time effective/visibility overlap is only causal when the PO was still invisible when the order line was actually written. Migrated legacy lines written after PO visibility are correlations, not recoverable backdating proof.

H1_post_cutoff_live_backdating.test: Match consumed items to durable backdated_ledger_events where effective_timestamp <= sale_time <= visibility_timestamp.

H2_post_cutoff_engine_drift.test: Inspect commits since 2026-07-02 in the four specified write/replay paths.
H2_post_cutoff_engine_drift.rationale: The only matching commit changes product/variant display-name lookup in an admin read path; it does not change MAC, consumption, or cost_at_sale writes.
relevant_commits: 5b315eb|2026-07-06T14:46:58+07:00|Antigravity fix: snapshot-first product/variant name lookup in orders (cache drift fix)

H3_pre_cutoff_baseline_scope_gap.test: Compare IDs and selection predicates. The same live audit has no sale-date filter; the frozen artifact is a point-in-time mismatch list.

H4_pre_baseline_window.test: Count UTC sale dates before the first date represented in the frozen baseline and before 2026-06-01.

H5_sign_asymmetry.test: Split expected-minus-stored delta by sign, product, shortfall BTP, and UTC sale date.
H5_sign_asymmetry.sign_semantics: delta_vnd = expected_cost - stored_cost; negative means stored COGS is higher than current replay, not under-stored.

H6_btp_shortfall_recurrence.test: Replay each line at sale time and inspect BTP_SHORTFALL consumption sources.

H7_edit_order_side_effects.test: Match EDITED events on the current order version with event_at later than the preserved sale timestamp.
H7_edit_order_side_effects.rationale: Current editOrderV2 explicitly recomputes and pins cost_at_sale at original sale time, so an EDITED event alone does not prove an unpinned write.
